name: farm_antibiotic_stewardship_restaurant_supply_contract
premise: Sustainability VP Alex Torres must decide whether to renew a restaurant chain contract with a poultry supplier after whistleblower photos suggest routine growth-promoter antibiotics banned in the chain's pledge. Supplier CEO Pike Irving privately shares veterinary records claiming therapeutic exemptions. Terminating the contract disrupts thirty percent of stores during a holiday protein shortage. Renewal preserves shelves but risks consumer fraud claims if exemptions were cosmetic. A national grocery chain is running surprise residue tests, and one failed lot could terminate the supply contract for the entire season. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Agricultural Policy.

agents.name: Alex Torres
agents.identity: I am the restaurant chain sustainability vice president accountable to public pledges, franchisee stability, and legal exposure on marketing claims. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want antibiotic stewardship honored without starving stores of protein during the holiday rush. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

agents.name: Pike Irving
agents.identity: I am the poultry supplier chief executive defending herd health decisions and confidentiality of vet treatment logs under producer agreements. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want the contract renewed while we complete an independent audit because abrupt termination bankrupts small growers in the pool. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

choices: I will terminate the supplier pool pending third-party audit and airfreight alternate certified birds at higher cost, accepting margin loss and some stockouts.
I will renew with a thirty-day holdback escrow and random slaughter-line antibiotic testing, accepting reputational harm if tests later fail publicly.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Franchisees are threatening a lawsuit if supply stops before Black Friday. Alex Torres must choose between contract termination with alternate sourcing or renewal with escrow testing. What does Alex Torres do?
